patches.summary: Adds StAppKey (ST-App-Key apiKey header) and TenantID fields plus ST_APP_KEY/ST_TENANT_ID env reads to Config.
patches.reason: ServiceTitan uses composed auth (static ST-App-Key apiKey header AND OAuth2 client_credentials bearer), but the v4.6.1 generator wired only the OAuth half (#1303 apiKey half). Every Memberships path is also tenant-scoped (/tenant/{tenant}/...), which needs a TenantID surface (#1332).
patches.validated_outcome: doctor reports 'configured (composed)' with auth_source=env:ST_APP_KEY; env_vars=OK 4/4 available

patches.summary: TrimSpace defensively around all four ST_* env reads (ST_CLIENT_ID, ST_CLIENT_SECRET, ST_APP_KEY, ST_TENANT_ID).
patches.reason: Trailing newlines from PowerShell/.env sourcing produced opaque invalid_client 400s from the ServiceTitan OAuth endpoint. Whitespace-stripping the values at the env-read boundary is the only place this can be fixed without leaking the credential bytes to logs (JKA env gotcha — sibling pricebook + inventory retro carry-forward).
patches.validated_outcome: auth login + live membership-types get-list succeed against the real JKA tenant

patches.summary: Wires the ST-App-Key header into every HTTP request (do() and the dry-run preview).
patches.reason: ServiceTitan rejects calls that present only the OAuth bearer; both halves of the composed auth must be on every request. The generator emitted the bearer half but not the apiKey half (#1303 apiKey half).
patches.validated_outcome: Phase 5 live smoke against JKA tenant returned real membership-type (id 67026827, 'Water Treatment Maintenance') with both Authorization and ST-App-Key headers

patches.summary: doctor recognizes composed auth as configured when ST_APP_KEY + ST_CLIENT_ID + ST_CLIENT_SECRET are all present, even before the bearer has been minted; also surfaces ST_TENANT_ID readiness.
patches.reason: The generator's doctor only recognized a bearer token as 'configured', so fresh installs of a composed-auth CLI always reported 'unconfigured' until the first call minted a token (#1303 / inventory-retro anti-pattern). Tenant readiness is part of the same readiness check (#1332).
patches.validated_outcome: doctor --json reports 'configured (composed: ST-App-Key + OAuth2 client_credentials; bearer minted on first call)' with env_vars=OK 4/4 available

patches.summary: Populates defaultSyncResources() and syncResourcePath() with the six cacheable Memberships v2 resources (memberships, membership-types, recurring-services, recurring-service-types, recurring-service-events, invoice-templates) and substitutes ST_TENANT_ID at call time.
patches.reason: The v4.6.1 generator emitted empty registry stubs, so `sync` was a no-op and the 12 novel audit/find commands had no local data to read (#1305). Every Memberships path is tenant-scoped, so {tenant} substitution is bundled in the same patch (#1332).
patches.validated_outcome: sync populates the local SQLite store; transcendence commands (renewals, expiring, overdue-events, drift, risk, revenue, health, stale-services, bill-preview, find) read from it

patches.summary: Renewals, OverdueEvents, Schedule, Risk, and StaleServices truncate `time.Now()` to the start of the UTC day before comparing against parsed ServiceTitan date timestamps.
patches.reason: Phase 4.95 native code review (5 high-severity findings). ServiceTitan timestamps parse as midnight UTC; comparing against `time.Now()` at any time-of-day other than midnight silently excluded memberships and events whose date equals 'today'. The audits now agree on day-boundary semantics across runs.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/memberships/... — 5 tests / 11 subtests pass; same-day renewals and overdue events now surface correctly

patches.summary: Removed per-row round2() from Revenue accumulators; rounding now happens once when the bucket is emitted.
patches.reason: Phase 4.95 native code review (medium-severity). Per-row rounding accumulates sub-cent error across large monthly buckets. End-of-bucket rounding is the standard fix.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/memberships/... clean; revenue rollups now have consistent cent-precision

patches.summary: lookupDurationBilling returns ok=false when no exact (duration, billingFrequency) or frequency-only match exists, instead of silently falling back to entries[0].
patches.reason: Greptile review (PR #601, P1). The 'last resort' return-first-entry made the !ok branch dead in callers, so a membership whose type carries multiple duration-billing entries with no matching frequency could get billed at the wrong tier. With ok=false, bill-preview can honestly report 'no matching duration-billing entry for this combination' instead of fabricating a number.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / vet / test clean; bill-preview reports missing-config honestly

patches.summary: Removed the unreachable third `if tokenURL == ""` guard in mintClientCredentials.
patches.reason: Greptile review (PR #601, P2). The block immediately above unconditionally sets tokenURL to the hardcoded ServiceTitan endpoint when empty, so the third guard's early-return path is dead.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / vet clean; one fewer mislead-future-maintainers code path
